items.title: Crypto Firms Race to 'Quantum-Proof' Wallets Before Bitcoin, Ethereum Networks Catch Up
items.desc: Crypto companies are upgrading wallets to counter the coming quantum computing threat, but gaps remain.

items.title: Ripple Prime Secures $200M Debt Facility to Expand Lending Capacity
items.desc: Funds managed by Neuberger Specialty Finance committed the facility to grow margin financing for the multi-asset prime broker.

items.title: Galaxy, Sharplink plan $125M institutional DeFi yield fund backed by ETH treasury
items.desc: Sharplink will contribute $100 million in staked Ether to a Galaxy-managed fund as institutions seek to earn yield from crypto holdings without selling ETH.

items.title: Stream Finance Breaks Six Month Silence With Wind-Down Plan
items.desc: A newly formed Delaware entity will consolidate and liquidate remaining assets, with "strategic alternatives" coming in the next few weeks.

items.title: Banks Sound Alarm as Senate Prepares CLARITY Act Markup
items.desc: The Senate Banking Committee is set to mark up the sweeping crypto market structure bill on Thursday.

items.title: Sharplink Q1 revenue tops $12 million as it pursues ‘singles and doubles’ hits in ETH yield
items.desc: Sharplink said its new Galaxy Digital partnership will deploy roughly $125 million into onchain liquidity and DeFi opportunities.

items.title: Galaxy to Manage $125M DeFi Yield Fund Seeded by Sharplink's ETH Treasury
items.desc: Mike Novogratz's firm will pick protocols and size exposures while Sharplink keeps its core staked ETH position intact.

items.title: Galaxy-backed Boundary to launch ‘verifiable’ institutional stablecoin USBD
items.desc: The project has raised $2 million in pre-seed funding led by Galaxy Ventures and plans to launch USBD on Ethereum in early summer 2026.

items.title: Anchorage is stepping back from Robinhood and Kraken-backed stablecoin group
items.desc: Anchorage Digital co-founder and CEO Nathan McCauley said there will be ‘increased neutrality’ from his firm on stablecoins.

items.title: Why the TradFi takeover of crypto might not be the death blow analysts expect
items.desc: Gate’s CBO argues that Bloomberg’s warning of a TradFi takeover is "oversimplified," noting that global exchanges evolved past fee-only models years ago.

items.title: Ethereum Firm Sharplink Posts $686 Million Loss, Kicks Off $125M Yield Fund With Galaxy
items.desc: Sharplink posted a quarterly net loss of nearly $686 million, but shares are up following word of a $125 million collaboration with Galaxy.

items.title: American Bankers Attempt Last Ditch Effort To Kill Crypto Market Structure Bill Regarding Stablecoins
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine American Bankers Attempt Last Ditch Effort To Kill Crypto Market Structure Bill Regarding Stablecoins American Bankers Association CEO Rob Nichols urged bank leaders to lobby against a stablecoin yield provision in the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act ahead of Thursday’s Senate markup. This post American Bankers Attempt Last Ditch Effort To Kill Crypto Market Structure Bill Regarding Stablecoins first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Tom Lee’s BitMine Slows Ethereum Buying Pace, Adding $62 Million in ETH
items.desc: Ethereum treasury firm BitMine Immersion Technologies slowed its ETH purchase pace, adding just $62 million worth last week.

items.title: Circle Raises $222M in Arc Presale at $3B FDV
items.desc: The private token sale for Circle's Arc blockchain was led by a16z crypto, and included BlackRock, Apollo, and Intercontinental Exchange.

items.title: Bank of England Chief Flags ‘Coming Wrestle' With US on Stablecoin Oversight
items.desc: The BoE Governor warned U.S. dollar tokens could flood Britain in a crisis, given the gap in redemption guarantees under the GENIUS Act.

items.title: Banking groups escalate fight over stablecoin yield ahead of Senate vote
items.desc: The American Bankers Association amplified warnings the Senate’s Clarity Act could push deposit flight into stablecoins unless lawmakers tighten yield limits.

items.title: Corpay taps BVNK to bring stablecoin wallets to corporate payments
items.desc: Corpay will use stablecoin rails in its treasury operations to improve capital efficiency, reduce reliance on pre-funded accounts, and move funds across its global footprint.

items.title: Senate Schedules CLARITY Act Markup as Banking Lobby, Democrats Mount Resistance
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Senate Schedules CLARITY Act Markup as Banking Lobby, Democrats Mount Resistance After nearly a year of delays, the Senate Banking Committee scheduled a markup of the CLARITY Act for May 14. This post Senate Schedules CLARITY Act Markup as Banking Lobby, Democrats Mount Resistance first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: The biggest consensus overhaul in Solana history is officially live for testing
items.desc: The upgrade, known as Alpenglow, is live on a community test cluster, said Solana core developer Anza.

items.title: American Bankers Association CEO makes final-hour push for tightened limits on stablecoin rewards ahead of Senate committee vote
items.desc: Days before a key Senate committee to vote on crypto legislation, ABA CEO Rob Nichols sent a letter to bank executives.

items.title: ‘Groundbreaking’ prediction market ETFs launch delayed again as SEC reviews more: analyst
items.desc: ETFs would offer investors a way to wager on the outcome of elections and economic events using the publicly-traded financial instruments.

items.title: Ripple Lands $200 Million Financing Facility to Expand Institutional Crypto Brokerage
items.desc: The XRP-linked Ripple secures financing from Neuberger Specialty Finance to fuel growth of its prime brokerage platform.

items.title: Strategy resumes Bitcoin acquisitions with $43M BTC buy
items.desc: Strategy added $43 million in Bitcoin last week after Michael Saylor drew mixed investor reactions for signaling potential BTC sales to fund dividend payments.

items.title: Ripple raises $200 million from Neuberger Berman to expand its Ripple Prime platform
items.desc: Ripple announced its agreement with the global investment firm Monday, noting that Ripple Prime’s revenue has tripled in the last year, when. it acquired it for $1.25 billion.

items.title: From hack to OP Stack: Ronin to migrate from gaming sidechain to Ethereum Layer 2 four years after Lazarus attack
items.desc: Ronin migrates from an Ethereum sidechain to an OP Stack Layer 2 on May 12, four years after Lazarus hackers stole over $600M in crypto.

items.title: ‘Crypto spring has commenced,’ Tom Lee says as Bitmine’s weekly accumulation slows below 100,000 ETH pace
items.desc: Bitmine Immersion bought another 26,659 ETH ($62 million) last week and now holds more than 5.2 million ETH in total.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.desc: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Neuberger backs Ripple Prime with $200 million debt line for margin lending expansion: Bloomberg
items.desc: Ripple Prime secured a $200 million Neuberger Berman facility to expand margin lending across equities, fixed income and crypto.

items.title: Strategy Resumes Bitcoin Buys as Saylor Shifts Focus to 'Never Be a Net Seller'
items.desc: The treasury firm resumed Bitcoin buys after last week's pause, with Michael Saylor saying that Strategy would buy 30 BTC for every one sold.

items.title: USDC issuer Circle raises $222M in Arc token presale valued at $3B
items.desc: Circle raised $222 million in an ARC token presale led by a16z Crypto as Q1 revenue hit $694 million and USDC circulation climbed to $77 billion.

items.title: Tom Lee's Bitmine slows down ether purchases after already buying 1 million tokens this year
items.desc: The firm bought roughly 26,000 ether last week, a sharp decline from the average of the previous weeks' 100,000.

items.title: XRP metrics line up bull signals for ‘full-scale rally’ to $2
items.desc: XRP is giving hints that a price breakout may be underway, based on several technical and onchain indicators, with bulls eyeing $2 as the first stop.

items.title: Morning Minute: The Clarity Act Markup Comes May 14
items.desc: The Clarity Act finally has a real markup date, while BlackRock is making its biggest tokenization push yet.

items.title: ‘Crypto at the crossroads’: Analysts say Iran tensions and whale selling cloud outlook as bitcoin holds above $81K
items.desc: Bitcoin holds above $81K as Iran rejects US peace terms, Brent tops $104, and a $1B whale's ETH selling clouds an otherwise bullish backdrop.

items.title: Circle Raises $222M in Arc Token Presale at $3B Valuation
items.desc: The USDC issuer announced the raise, with investors including Andreessen Horowitz and Blackrock, alongside its Q1 results.

items.title: Strategy buys 535 bitcoin for $43 million days after signaling potential BTC sales
items.desc: Last week's purchases were funded by sales of the company's common stock.

items.title: Ronin set to transition to Ethereum layer 2 from independent sidechain
items.desc: The purpose behind the migration is to enhance security, tokenomics and scalability, said Ronin, which suffered the largest DeFi bridge exploit on record in 2022.

items.title: ‘Back to work’: Michael Saylor’s Strategy buys 535 bitcoin for $43 million after brief pause as total holdings hit 818,869 BTC
items.desc: Strategy's total holdings now account for more than 3.9% of the 21 million bitcoin supply cap — worth around $66.5 billion.

items.title: S&P 500 payments firm Corpay taps BVNK to add stablecoin wallets for global customers
items.desc: Corpay partnered with BVNK to add stablecoin wallets and 24/7 settlement across its 800,000-client global payments network.

items.title: Circle raises $222M in Arc token presale at $3B FDV from a16z crypto, BlackRock and others; Q1 revenue up 20%
items.desc: Circle raised $222M in an Arc presale at a $3B valuation; BlackRock, Apollo, and a16z invest as Q1 revenue reaches $694M and USDC hits $77B.

items.title: Crypto funds log $858M in sixth straight week of inflows: CoinShares
items.desc: Crypto ETPs drew $858 million in inflows last week, led by Bitcoin with $706 million, as sentiment improved and short-BTC funds saw $14 million of outflows.

items.title: Crypto Funds Add $858M as Clarity Act Drives Market Optimism
items.desc: Digital asset inflows peak at a six-week high as a Senate stablecoin deal stokes institutional appetite across Bitcoin and altcoins.

items.title: Bitcoin funds capture $700 million as institutions place their bets
items.desc: Your day-ahead look for May 11, 2026

items.title: Michael Saylor’s latest tax strategy echoes Strategy’s 2022 bitcoin sale
items.desc: Michael Saylor confirmed the company was prepared to sell bitcoin, reviving a tax loss harvesting strategy first used in 2022.

items.title: Circle raises $222 million for Arc, beats Q1 earnings estimates but misses on revenue
items.desc: Circle raised $222 million for its Arc blockchain token at a $3 billion valuation, drawing backing from BlackRock, Apollo and Bullish.

items.title: Bitcoin whipsaws on CME open as Iran tensions pressure crypto markets
items.desc: BTC briefly topped $82,400 before slipping below $81,000 as traders repositioned around CME futures open and geopolitical risks rattled markets.

items.title: Canton Network’s Digital Asset targets $2 billion valuation in a16z crypto-led raise: Bloomberg
items.desc: Sources familiar with the matter told Bloomberg the fundraiser is expected to close in the coming weeks and the final amount might vary.

items.title: Capital B raises $17.8M to expand its Bitcoin treasury
items.desc: Capital B raised $17.8 million from investors, including Adam Back and TOBAM, saying proceeds could help add 182 BTC to its treasury.

items.title: Global crypto funds log $858M inflows in sixth straight positive week as Clarity Act progress lifts sentiment: CoinShares
items.desc: Crypto fund inflows hit a sixth straight positive week, led by U.S. products and bitcoin ETFs, amid Clarity Act optimism, per CoinShares.

items.title: This Bitcoin price level will be 'end of the bears' if broken, says analyst
items.desc: Bitcoin’s rejection at the 200-day EMA mirrors past 25% and 36% BTC sell-offs, fueling fears of another drop toward $60,000.

items.title: Australia to propose capital gains tax changes affecting crypto investors: reports
items.desc: The government plans to replace a 50% capital gains tax discount on assets held for more than one year, according to AFR.

items.title: Bitcoin, Nasdaq investors are celebrating, while U.S. consumers turn gloomy
items.desc: Bitcoin and Nasdaq have rallied sharply, but U.S. consumer sentiment has fallen to historic lows, highlighting a widening Wall Street–Main Street divide.

items.title: French BTC treasury firm Capital B raises $18 million from Adam Back, others
items.desc: Capital B announced that it has raised 15.2 million euros in a private placement, which will be used to purchase 182 BTC.

items.title: New Fed chair, Base's Azul upgrade, corporate earnings: Crypto Week Ahead
items.desc: Your look at what's coming in the week starting May 11.

items.title: 7 Democrats seen as ‘key’ to advancing CLARITY Act: Galaxy
items.desc: The CLARITY Act, introduced in July 2025, stalled in January after Coinbase withdrew its support for the legislation over concerns about legal protections and stablecoin yields.

items.title: Australia plans capital gains tax changes affecting crypto investors: Report
items.desc: The Albanese government’s budget plans to replace the 50% capital gains tax discount on assets held over 12 months with a model taxing full real gains adjusted for inflation.

items.title: White hat returns $190K to Renegade hours after hacking the protocol
items.desc: The white hat hacker said the decision to exploit Renegade’s dark pool was made to protect the funds and safety of DeFi users.

items.title: Bank of England chief says global stablecoin rules will ‘wrestle’ with US
items.desc: Global regulators will have to confront US policy on dollar-backed stablecoins as they seek international standards for payment tokens, Andrew Bailey said.

items.title: Bitcoin mining pools with 75% of BTC hashrate join open standard for block construction
items.desc: Foundry, AntPool, F2Pool, SpiderPool, and MARA Pool are among seven pools joining the Stratum V2 working group, putting nearly three-quarters of global bitcoin hashrate behind a protocol that returns block construction decisions to individual miners.

items.title: SUI spikes 50% amid staking moves, zero-fee stablecoins, privacy push
items.desc: Adeniyi Abiodun, co-founder of Mysten Labs, announced at an event in Miami plans for zero-fee stablecoin transfers on Sui and reiterated the ambition to add private transactions.

items.title: Privacy advocates slam reCAPTCHA update they say locks out de-Googled phones
items.desc: Privacy-conscious internet users are being “demoted” from second to third-class netizens, said Bitcoiner Jameson Lopp.

items.title: XRP spikes 2.5%, beating bitcoin and ether, in breakout above $1.45
items.desc: Sharp move through resistance came with one of the biggest volume spikes in weeks, though profit-taking quickly emerged near session highs.

items.title: A bitcoin whale that went silent in 2013 moves $40 million in BTC
items.desc: A long-dormant whale woke up Sunday, moving millions worth of bitcoin on-chain.

items.title: Saylor signals another Bitcoin buy after hinting at selling in Q1 earnings call
items.desc: Saylor’s latest post suggests Strategy may resume buying after executives said the firm could sell BTC to fund dividends or offset taxes.

items.title: Policy at Consensus Miami: State of Crypto
items.desc: This was a hectic week.

items.title: Ether down 35% versus Bitcoin in a year: Will the ETH price downtrend continue?
items.desc: Ether's ongoing downtrend against Bitcoin mirrors the bearish structure seen in 2024–2025, raising the risk of another 40% decline.

items.title: Agentic commerce will run on crypto rails, PayPal and Google reps tell Consensus Miami
items.desc: Senior figures from PayPal and Google Cloud said open payment protocols, machine-readable merchant catalogs and multi-party crypto custody are needed to scale agentic commerce.

items.title: Canton Network creator targets $300M in capital raise: Report
items.desc: Bloomberg reported Digital Asset Holdings is raising money at a $2B valuation in a round led by a16z Crypto, which comes less than a year after an earlier nine-figure round.

items.title: Bored Ape NFTs are finally making a comeback as crypto traders rediscover their appetite for risk
items.desc: BAYC floor prices have doubled in a month as traders rotate back into speculative crypto bets, reviving hopes of a broader NFT recovery.

items.title: 3 young DeFi apps return $100M in revenue to token holders in 30 days
items.desc: Hyperliquid, EdgeX and Pump.fun returned a combined $96 million to token holders in 30 days, as the crypto community shifts its focus from transaction volumes to real earnings.

items.title: CVE-2024-52911 - Script Interpreter Remote Crash
items.desc: After Bitcoin Core 0.14.0 and before Bitcoin Core 29.0, validating a specially-crafted block may cause the node to access previously freed memory. During validation, necessary data required for checking inputs for each transaction is pre-calculated and cached. For specially crafted invalid blocks, it was possible for this data to be destroyed while it was still being accessed by a background validation thread. An attacker capable of mining a block with sufficient proof-of-work could have exploited this to crash victim nodes. Because of the nature of use-after-free bugs, it is possible that the crash could have been used for remote code execution, though constraints on the input (block) data make this unlikely. This issue is considered High severity. Details By default, script validation for new blocks is dispatched to background threads via a vector of CScriptCheck functors. Each CScriptCheck holds a pointer to a PrecomputedTransactionData object which stores some data needed by each input in the transaction. Because it stores a pointer and not the data itself, care must be taken to ensure that the PrecomputedTransactionData outlives the CScriptCheck. The script checks lifetime is enforced by an RAII class, CCheckQueueControl. However, the control is intantiated before the precomputed transaction data. Because local objects in C++ are destructed in reverse order of construction, this means the vector of PrecomputedTransactionData is destroyed before the CCheckQueueControl. This is not an issue when the block is valid, as CCheckQueueControl::Wait() will be called before the function returns and the PrecomputedTransactionData gets destroyed. However, in case of an early return (when a separate check fails) a background script thread may read the precomputed transaction data after it was destroyed. An attacker could exploit this to crash victim nodes at the expense of a valid PoW at tip. Attribution Cory Fields (MIT DCI) discovered this vulnerability and responsibly disclosed it in a detailed report containing a proof of concept for reproduction and a proposed mitigation. Timeline 2024-11-02 Cory Fields privately reports the bug 2024-11-06 Pieter Wuille pushes a covert fix to already open PR #31112 which works around the issue by removing the early returns 2024-12-03 PR #31112 is merged 2025-04-12 Bitcoin Core version 29.0 is released with a fix 2026-04-19 The last vulnerable Bitcoin Core version (28.x) goes end of life 2026-05-05 Public disclosure.

items.title: Bitcoin Core 31.0 released
items.desc: Bitcoin Core version 31.0 is now available for download. See the release notes for more information about the bug fixes in this release. If you have any questions, please stop by the #bitcoin IRC chatroom (IRC, web) and we’ll do our best to help you.

items.title: Bitcoin Core 31.0 released
items.desc: Bitcoin Core version 31.0 is now available for download. See the release notes for more information about the bug fixes in this release. If you have any questions, please stop by the #bitcoin IRC chatroom (IRC, web) and we’ll do our best to help you.
